evals.prompt: I'm starting work on a new feature. Run pulse:preflight to check whether my environment is ready for the Pulse workflow.
evals.expected_output: Agent checks git, br, bv, and coordination runtime availability; writes .pulse/tooling-status.json with PASS/DEGRADED/FAIL and a recommended_mode; presents a clear status summary.
expectations: Agent attempts to check for git, br, and bv tool availability
Agent writes or describes writing .pulse/tooling-status.json
Agent reports a recommended_mode (swarm, single-worker, planning-only, or blocked)
Agent does NOT immediately start implementing features or creating beads

evals.prompt: I just ran preflight and it passed. I want to add a dark mode toggle to our React app. What Pulse skill should I use next and why?
evals.expected_output: Agent consults the skill catalog, identifies the request as a new feature with fuzzy decisions, routes to pulse:brainstorming or pulse:exploring, and explains the routing decision with the go-mode pipeline context.
expectations: Agent routes the user to pulse:brainstorming or pulse:exploring as the next step
Agent references the Pulse skill catalog or routing table
Agent does NOT jump directly to planning or executing
Agent explains why the chosen skill is appropriate for a new feature request

evals.prompt: I want to add a notification system to my app. Help me brainstorm how to approach this.
evals.expected_output: Agent asks ONE clarifying question at a time (not a batch), eventually proposes 2-3 approaches with trade-offs, presents a design for approval, writes a spec doc, runs self-review, and hands off to pulse:exploring. Does NOT write code.
expectations: Agent asks clarifying questions one at a time, not in a batch
Agent proposes 2-3 distinct approaches with trade-offs
Agent does NOT write any code or create beads during brainstorming
Agent identifies the terminal state as handing off to pulse:exploring

evals.prompt: I want to add user authentication with OAuth to our Node.js API. Let's explore what decisions we need to lock before planning.
evals.expected_output: Agent classifies scope, asks sequential Socratic questions (one at a time), assigns decision IDs (D1, D2...), and writes history/<feature>/CONTEXT.md. Does NOT start planning or create beads.
expectations: Agent asks questions one at a time with a HARD-GATE between each
Agent assigns stable decision IDs (D1, D2, etc.) as decisions are locked
Agent outputs or describes writing history/<feature>/CONTEXT.md
Agent does NOT create beads, write code, or invoke pulse:planning prematurely

evals.prompt: Make this prompt better: 'fix the login bug'
evals.expected_output: Agent transforms the vague prompt into a structured, execution-ready version with Objective, Context, Tool Rules, Output Contract, and Done Criteria blocks. Explains what changed.
expectations: Agent produces a significantly more structured and detailed version of the input prompt
Agent includes at least 3 of the framework blocks (Objective, Context, Tool Rules, Output Contract, Verification, Done Criteria)
Agent explains what was changed and why
Output is clearly more actionable than the original 'fix the login bug'

evals.prompt: Use pulse:gkg to find all files related to authentication in the current codebase and give me an architecture snapshot.
evals.expected_output: Agent checks .pulse/tooling-status.json for gkg readiness, uses gkg primitives (or rg fallback), summarizes architecture findings, and notes where results are saved (discovery.md).
expectations: Agent checks for gkg availability before running commands
Agent uses gkg primitives (map, search, refs) or falls back to rg if unavailable
Agent produces an architecture summary or discovery notes
Agent saves or describes saving findings to history/<feature>/discovery.md

evals.prompt: Exploring is complete. CONTEXT.md is at history/auth-oauth/CONTEXT.md. Now run pulse:planning to research the codebase and produce a phase plan for the OAuth feature.
evals.expected_output: Agent reads CONTEXT.md and critical-patterns.md, runs discovery, writes approach.md and phase-plan.md, waits for human approval of the phase plan, then writes current-phase contract/story map and beads using `br`.
expectations: Agent reads CONTEXT.md before doing anything else
Agent checks history/learnings/critical-patterns.md for institutional learnings
Agent produces or describes producing a phase-plan.md and approach.md
Agent pauses for human approval before creating beads for the current phase

evals.prompt: Planning is done. The phase contract and story map are written. Run pulse:validating to verify the current phase is execution-ready.
evals.expected_output: Agent enforces the bead schema, runs the plan-checker across 8 dimensions, executes spike beads for HIGH-risk items, polishes beads with bv, asks exit-readiness questions, and requires explicit user approval before saying execution can start.
expectations: Agent checks that required bead fields (files, verify, dependencies) are present
Agent runs or describes a plan-checker verification across multiple dimensions
Agent requires explicit user approval before declaring execution-ready
Agent does NOT start implementing beads itself

evals.prompt: Validating passed and execution is approved. Run pulse:swarming to coordinate the worker agents for this phase.
evals.expected_output: Agent confirms swarm readiness, initializes coordination runtime, spawns worker subagents running pulse:executing, enters a poll-act monitor loop, and never directly implements beads itself.
expectations: Agent initializes or describes initializing the coordination runtime (project/agent registration)
Agent spawns worker subagents running pulse:executing (not implementing beads directly)
Agent enters a monitoring loop checking worker inbox and topic
Agent updates or describes updating .pulse/STATE.md with worker tracking

evals.prompt: I'm a worker agent. Run pulse:executing in single-worker mode to implement the next available bead.
evals.expected_output: Agent determines it's in single-worker mode, reads bead graph via `bv --robot-priority`, claims the top bead, reads it fully including locked decisions, implements it respecting TDD, runs verification commands, commits per-bead, and loops.
expectations: Agent determines execution mode (single-worker vs swarm worker)
Agent uses bv --robot-priority (or describes using it) to claim the next bead
Agent reads the full bead including learning_refs before implementing
Agent commits after each bead and writes verification evidence

evals.prompt: Execution is complete for the OAuth feature. Run pulse:reviewing to verify quality before merge.
evals.expected_output: Agent runs 4-5 specialist review agents (code quality, architecture, security, tests, synthesis), converts findings to P1/P2/P3 review beads, performs artifact verification, runs UAT against CONTEXT.md decisions, and blocks merge on P1s.
expectations: Agent spawns or describes spawning multiple specialist review agents (at least code quality, security, tests)
Agent creates review beads for findings with P1/P2/P3 severity
Agent performs artifact verification (checking verification files exist and are substantive)
Agent blocks merge if P1 beads exist and requires them to be resolved first

evals.prompt: The OAuth feature is merged. Run pulse:compounding to capture what we learned.
evals.expected_output: Agent gathers context from feature history/beads/reviews, runs pattern/decision/failure analysis, writes learning files under history/learnings/, promotes only global rules to critical-patterns.md, and updates STATE.md.
expectations: Agent gathers context from feature history, review findings, and debug notes
Agent analyzes through pattern, decision, and failure lenses
Agent writes learning files to history/learnings/ with domain/severity/propagation metadata
Agent only promotes genuinely global patterns to critical-patterns.md, not everything

evals.prompt: A worker is stuck: the OAuth callback test is failing with 'Cannot read property token of undefined'. Debug this with pulse:debugging.
evals.expected_output: Agent classifies the issue (test failure), checks bead learning_refs first, reproduces the failure with the exact command, traces root cause, writes a debug note, and hands off to compounding for learning propagation.
expectations: Agent classifies the issue type before starting (test failure vs build vs runtime)
Agent checks bead-local learning_refs before doing a global search
Agent identifies and states a clear root cause sentence
Agent writes a debug note to .pulse/debug-notes/ and recommends compounding

evals.prompt: There are 3 known bugs in the payment module: (1) double-charge on retry, (2) webhook signature validation failing on some providers, (3) refund amount rounding error. Fix them systematically.
evals.expected_output: Agent creates a tracker with all 3 issues, sequences them by dependency, investigates each before fixing, verifies each fix with a narrow test, adds regression coverage, and reports status for all items.
expectations: Agent creates an issue tracker listing all 3 bugs with IDs and status
Agent investigates (reproduces + traces root cause) before applying any fix
Agent applies fixes one at a time and verifies each individually
Agent adds regression tests or coverage for each fixed bug

evals.prompt: Review and simplify my recent code changes. Run pulse:simplify-code in review-only mode.
evals.expected_output: Agent determines scope (git diff or specified files), spawns 4 parallel review sub-agents (reuse, quality, efficiency, clarity), aggregates structured findings with file/line/category/fix, and reports a summary without making any changes.
expectations: Agent determines the review scope from git diff or explicit files
Agent launches multiple specialist review sub-agents or lenses (at least 2 of: reuse, quality, efficiency, clarity)
Agent aggregates findings with file, line, category, and suggested fix
Agent does NOT modify any files in review-only mode

evals.prompt: I want to create a new Pulse skill called 'pulse:dependency-audit' that automatically audits npm dependencies for security issues. Use pulse:writing-pulse-skills to develop it with TDD.
evals.expected_output: Agent runs RED phase (define purpose, craft pressure scenarios, run without skill to capture rationalizations), then GREEN phase (write minimal SKILL.md addressing those rationalizations), then validates. Documents in CREATION-LOG.md.
expectations: Agent runs a RED phase: defines the skill purpose and creates pressure scenarios to expose failure modes
Agent runs a GREEN phase: writes a SKILL.md that addresses the documented rationalizations
Agent verifies the skill by rerunning pressure scenarios after writing
Agent documents the TDD process in a CREATION-LOG.md

evals.prompt: Run pulse:dream to consolidate learnings from recent Pulse work into the knowledge base.
evals.expected_output: Agent detects bootstrap vs recurring mode, selects Codex sources, extracts durable candidates, classifies each (match/ambiguous/no-match), updates learning files with provenance, stores ambiguous items in dream-pending/, and never auto-edits critical-patterns.md without approval.
expectations: Agent detects whether this is a bootstrap-first or recurring consolidation run
Agent extracts learning candidates and classifies them (match/ambiguous/no-match)
Agent writes provenance markers when updating learning files
Agent does NOT auto-edit critical-patterns.md — requires explicit approval for promotions

evals.prompt: I have a screenshot of our app's dashboard (dashboard.png). Use pulse:ai-multimodal to analyze it and extract all visible UI text and component labels.
evals.expected_output: Agent checks for GEMINI_API_KEY and Gemini setup, uses gemini_batch_process.py or the analyze script on the image, extracts all visible text/labels, and returns structured results.
expectations: Agent checks for GEMINI_API_KEY or Gemini setup before proceeding
Agent uses or describes using a bundled Gemini script (gemini_batch_process.py or similar) rather than just reading the file natively
Agent returns structured extracted text/labels from the image
Agent mentions rate limits or format constraints relevant to image processing
